title: clinopodium vulgare var. neogaeum
intro: Clinopodium vulgare var. neogaeum, also known as Mountain Mint, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and distinctive minty fragrance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its carminative and antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve digestive issues, such as indigestion, bloating, and cramps, as well as its anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Mountain Mint include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for headaches and fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Clinopodium vulgare var. neogaeum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
